<!-- Generated by AgentReady (LLM-first mode) | Dynamic section: do not edit manually. Static section in agent-context.json: safe to edit. -->

# Agent Working Memory Schema — Food Delivery Platform

This file defines the structured working memory an AI agent should maintain across a coding session on this polyglot microservices codebase. Each section below provides the YAML schema, field-level comments, and platform-specific guidance derived from the actual repository structure.

---

## 1. `session_state` — Current Task Tracking

Tracks where the agent is right now: what it was asked to do, which service it is operating in, and what phase of the work cycle it has reached.

```yaml
session_state:
  task_id: ""                    # Short human-readable identifier, e.g. "add-driver-rating-endpoint"
  task_description: ""           # Full description of the current task as given by the user
  status: ""                     # One of: planning | in_progress | blocked | awaiting_review | complete
  phase: ""                      # One of: analysis | scaffolding | implementation | testing | cleanup

  active_service: ""
  # Which microservice module is the primary focus of this session.
  # Must be one of: api-gateway | order-service | inventory-service | driver-service | notification-service
  # Critical: each service has its own toolchain — never cross-apply build commands.
  #   api-gateway        → Kotlin/Gradle, Spring Cloud Gateway (reactive/WebFlux)
  #   order-service      → Java/Maven, Spring Boot, JPA/Hibernate, Stripe, RabbitMQ
  #   inventory-service  → Python/pip, FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy + asyncpg, Redis locks
  #   driver-service     → Go/go mod, raw net/http, MongoDB
  #   notification-service → TypeScript/npm, Node.js, amqplib, Twilio, SendGrid

  active_language: ""            # Dominant language in scope: Java | Kotlin | Python | Go | TypeScript
  active_framework: ""           # e.g. "Spring Boot", "FastAPI", "net/http"

  entry_point_confirmed: false
  # Set true once the agent has verified the correct entry point for the active service:
  #   order-service → OrderServiceApplication.java
  #   api-gateway   → GatewayApplication.kt
  #   inventory-service → inventory-service/main.py
  #   driver-service → driver-service/cmd/driver/main.go
  #   notification-service → notification-service/src/index.ts

  public_paths_reviewed: false
  # IMPORTANT for api-gateway work: any new public (unauthenticated) route MUST be added to
  # the PUBLIC_PATHS list in JwtAuthFilter.kt (filter runs at order -100).
  # Set true once this check has been performed for the current task.

  order_status_machine_reviewed: false
  # Set true if the task touches order lifecycle. OrderStatus.isValidTransition() enforces
  # all transitions — invalid moves throw IllegalStateException.
  # Valid path: PENDING → ACCEPTED → PREPARING → READY_FOR_PICKUP → IN_TRANSIT → DELIVERED
  # Side-exits: CANCELLED, REFUNDED

  stripe_capture_mode_noted: false
  # Set true if the task touches payment flow. The order-service uses MANUAL capture —
  # PaymentIntents are authorized but NOT captured until order status progresses.
  # stripePaymentIntentId (pi_xxxx) is stored on the Order entity.

  redis_lock_scope_noted: false
  # Set true if the task touches inventory stock reservation.
  # acquire_lock() in inventory-service must be mocked or a real Redis instance provided in tests.

  last_completed_step: ""        # Freeform — last atomic action successfully completed
  next_planned_step: ""          # Freeform — immediate next action the agent intends to take
  blocking_reason: ""            # Populated only when status == "blocked"; describes the blocker
  session_start_context: ""      # Brief summary of repo state at session start (branch, recent changes, etc.)
```

---

## 2. `decisions_made` — Technical Decision Log

Every non-trivial design or implementation choice should be recorded here with its rationale. This prevents revisiting the same decision in later turns and provides an audit trail for human review.

```yaml
decisions_made:
  - decision_id: ""              # Unique short ID, e.g. "D-001"
    timestamp: ""                # ISO-8601 or relative, e.g. "2024-07-15T10:32:00Z"
    service: ""                  # Affected microservice module (see active_service values above)
    component: ""
    # The specific class/file/function affected. Use fully qualified names for Java/Kotlin:
    #   e.g. "com.fooddelivery.order.service.OrderService"
    #   e.g. "com.fooddelivery.gateway.JwtAuthFilter"
    #   e.g. "inventory-service/main.py::reserve_stock"
    #   e.g. "driver-service/cmd/driver/main.go::ServeHTTP"

    decision: ""                 # One sentence: what was decided
    alternatives_considered:     # List of other options that were rejected
      - ""
    rationale: ""
    # Explain WHY. Reference platform constraints where relevant, e.g.:
    #   - "Chose async def because inventory-service uses async SQLAlchemy with asyncpg;
    #      synchronous patterns will deadlock the event loop."
    #   - "Used @Transactional on OrderService method because JPA lazy-loading requires
    #      an active session for OrderItem embeddables."
    #   - "Routed through api-gateway PUBLIC_PATHS rather than creating a bypass because
    #      removing JwtAuthFilter checks is a forbidden operation."
    #   - "Used Redis SETNX via acquire_lock() rather than a DB lock to prevent stock
    #      overselling across concurrent FastAPI async workers."

    trade_offs: ""               # Acknowledged downsides of the chosen approach
    reversible: true             # Whether this decision can be easily undone
    requires_human_approval: false
    # Set true if decision touches any forbidden operation area:
    #   JwtAuthFilter security | Stripe payment flow | openapi.yaml | CI/CD | .env secrets
    #   database migration scripts | docker-compose infrastructure | lock files
    approval_status: ""          # One of: pending | approved | rejected | not_required
    linked_files:                # Files whose content reflects this decision
      - ""
```

---

## 3. `files_modified` — Change Tracking

One entry per file touched in the session. Recording the *reason* alongside the *diff summary* is critical in a polyglot monorepo where a change in one service may require a coordinated change in another (e.g. a new RabbitMQ event shape published by order-service must be consumed correctly by notification-service).

```yaml
files_modified:
  - path: ""
    # Repo-relative path, e.g.:
    #   "order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/service/OrderService.java"
    #   "inventory-service/main.py"
    #   "driver-service/cmd/driver/main.go"
    #   "notification-service/src/index.ts"
    #   "api-gateway/src/main/kotlin/com/fooddelivery/gateway/JwtAuthFilter.kt"

    service: ""                  # Parent microservice module
    language: ""                 # Java | Kotlin | Python | Go | TypeScript
    change_type: ""              # One of: created | modified | deleted | renamed
    reason: ""                   # WHY this file was changed (link to task or decision_id)

    diff_summary: ""
    # Concise human-readable summary of what changed inside the file.
    # For Java/Spring Boot: note any new @Bean, @Transactional, @RabbitListener, or @RestMapping added.
    # For Python/FastAPI: note any new async def route, SQLAlchemy model field, or Redis lock usage.
    # For Go: note any new handler registered in ServeHTTP path/method switch.
    # For TypeScript/notification: note any new amqplib channel.consume() binding or idempotency key change.
    # For Kotlin/gateway: note any PUBLIC_PATHS change or new RouteLocator bean.

    jpa_schema_impact: false
    # Java/Kotlin only. Set true if a JPA @Entity or @Embeddable was changed — may require
    # a Flyway/Liquibase migration or schema update in the order-service PostgreSQL database.

    api_contract_impact: false
    # Set true if the change alters a REST endpoint signature, request/response shape,
    # or HTTP status code. Reminder: openapi.yaml is restricted — flag for human review.

    rabbitmq_event_impact: false
    # Set true if the change alters the shape or routing key of events published to the
    # "order.events" RabbitMQ exchange. The notification-service consumer must stay in sync.

    stripe_flow_impact: false
    # Set true if the change touches Stripe PaymentIntent creation, manual capture,
    # or refund logic. Requires human review (forbidden operation zone).

    test_file_counterpart: ""    # Path to the corresponding test file, if one exists or was created
    build_command_to_verify: ""
    # The exact command to compile/verify this file's service after the change:
    #   order-service     → "cd order-service && mvn -B verify --no-transfer-progress"
    #   inventory-service → "cd inventory-service && pytest"
    #   driver-service    → "cd driver-service && go test ./..."
    #   notification-service → "cd notification-service && npm test"
    #   api-gateway       → "cd api-gateway && ./gradlew test"

    is_restricted_path: false
    # MUST be true (and change must be blocked) if path matches any restricted write path:
    #   .env | .env.example | driver-service/go.sum | notification-service/package-lock.json
    #   openapi.yaml | .github/workflows/ci.yml | .github/workflows/agentic-ready.yml
    #   .github/workflows/context-drift-detector.yml
```

---

## 4. `tests_run` — Test Execution Log

Records every test invocation during the session, including which framework was used and whether platform-specific infrastructure dependencies (Redis, RabbitMQ, MongoDB, Postgres) were available.

```yaml
tests_run:
  - run_id: ""                   # Short ID, e.g. "T-001"
    timestamp: ""                # When the test run occurred
    service: ""                  # Microservice under test
    framework: ""
    # Test framework for the active service:
    #   order-service      → JUnit 5 (+ Spring Boot Test, Mockito, Testcontainers)
    #   inventory-service  → pytest (+ pytest-asyncio for async SQLAlchemy routes)
    #   driver-service     → go test
    #   notification-service → Jest
    #   api-gateway        → JUnit 5 (+ Spring WebFlux Test — non-blocking)

    command: ""                  # Exact shell command executed
    scope: ""                    # One of: unit | integration | contract | full-suite

    infrastructure_mocked:
      postgres: false            # Was PostgreSQL mocked/containerized or assumed live?
      redis: false
      # Redis mock status — critical for inventory-service tests that call acquire_lock().
      # If false and Redis unavailable, stock reservation tests will fail.
      rabbitmq: false            # Was RabbitMQ mocked? Affects OrderService publish and notification consumer.
      mongodb: false             # Was MongoDB mocked? Affects driver-service handler tests.
      stripe: false              # Was Stripe API mocked? Affects OrderService payment tests.

    passed: 0                    # Count of passing tests
    failed: 0                    # Count of failing tests
    skipped: 0                   # Count of skipped tests
    total: 0

    coverage_percent: null       # Overall line/statement coverage if reported
